title: Add minimal visual instrumentation for tuning
overview: Create a debug overlay toggled by `?debug` query param that displays FPS, active mode name, point count, and bass/treble values. The overlay reads frame data from the render loop via a lightweight callback, renders into a fixed-position DOM element, and is completely disabled by default.

files_to_create.purpose: Debug overlay module: exports `createDebugOverlay()` that returns a DOM element and an `update(data)` method. The update method accepts a `DebugFrameData` object and writes formatted text into the overlay. FPS is computed from delta. No raw visitor identifiers are exposed.
key_details: Export interface DebugFrameData: { fps: number, modeName: string, pointCount: number, bass: number, treble: number }
Export function createDebugOverlay(): { element: HTMLElement, update: (data: DebugFrameData) => void }
Element uses class `eavi-debug-overlay` with fixed position top-left
Update throttled to ~4Hz (every ~250ms) to avoid layout thrash
Text-only, monospace font, semi-transparent background matching existing UI style

files_to_create.purpose: Tests for the debug overlay module covering creation, update behavior, content format, privacy, and CSS presence.

changes: Add optional `onDebugFrame` callback to `LoopDeps` interface: `onDebugFrame?: ((data: { fps: number, modeName: string, pointCount: number, bass: number, treble: number }) => void) | null`
At end of frame function (after render, before requestAnimationFrame), call `d.onDebugFrame?.({ fps: 1000/delta, modeName, pointCount, bass, treble })`
Mode name and point count need to come from deps — add optional `getModeName?: (() => string) | null` and `getPointCount?: (() => number) | null` to LoopDeps
If debug enabled: create overlay, append element to body, wire `deps.onDebugFrame` to `overlay.update`
After modeManager creation, set `deps.getModeName` and `deps.getPointCount` from modeManager and quality
Add `.eavi-debug-overlay` styles: fixed top-left, z-index 30, monospace font, small text, semi-transparent dark background, padding, pointer-events none, safe-area insets
Add prefers-reduced-motion rule for debug overlay (transition: none)

details: Define `DebugFrameData` interface with fps, modeName, pointCount, bass, treble
Implement `createDebugOverlay()` returning { element, update }
Element is a `<div>` with class `eavi-debug-overlay`, hidden attribute initially false
update() throttles to ~4Hz using a timestamp check
Format: multi-line text showing each value on its own line, e.g. `FPS: 60 | mode: pointcloud\npoints: 1200 | bass: 142 | treble: 38`
Round FPS to integer, bass/treble to integer (0-255 range)
No raw identifiers (IP, UA, geo, seed) are shown
Add `onDebugFrame?: ((data: { fps: number, modeName: string, pointCount: number, bass: number, treble: number }) => void) | null`
Add `getModeName?: (() => string) | null`
Add `getPointCount?: (() => number) | null`
After `renderer.render(scene, camera)`, invoke `d.onDebugFrame` with computed values
fps = delta > 0 ? 1000 / delta : 0
modeName from `d.getModeName?.() ?? 'loading'`
pointCount from `d.getPointCount?.() ?? 0`
position: fixed, top with safe-area-inset, left with safe-area-inset
z-index: 30 (above buttons at 10, below info overlay at 20 — actually make it 30 so it's always visible)
font-family: monospace, font-size: 0.7rem, color: rgba(255,255,255,0.7)
background: rgba(0,0,0,0.5), padding: 0.5rem 0.75rem, border-radius: 4px
pointer-events: none (don't interfere with scene interaction)
white-space: pre (preserve line breaks)
Add to prefers-reduced-motion rule
At top level (before startLoop), check `new URLSearchParams(window.location.search).has('debug')`
If true: call createDebugOverlay(), append element to document.body
Set `deps.onDebugFrame = overlay.update`
In geoPromise.then(), after modeManager creation, set deps.getModeName to return `modes[modeManager.activeIndex].name` (use the modes array from the createModeManager call)
Set deps.getPointCount to return the active mode's max point count from quality profile
Test: createDebugOverlay returns element and update function
Test: element has class eavi-debug-overlay
Test: update writes FPS, mode name, point count, bass, treble into text content
Test: overlay does not display raw identifiers (no IP, UA, seed, geo in text)
Test: CSS file contains .eavi-debug-overlay with position fixed
Test: CSS file contains pointer-events none for debug overlay
Test: no localStorage or cookie access (privacy check, matching existing pattern)
Test: update throttles (calling update rapidly doesn't update text every time)
Run `npm run build` — must succeed
Run `npm test` — all tests must pass
Manual check: load without ?debug — no overlay visible
Manual check: load with ?debug — overlay shows updating values

unit_tests: createDebugOverlay returns correct structure (element + update fn)
update() populates text content with all required fields
No forbidden storage APIs used (localStorage, cookies)
Throttle behavior: rapid calls don't update every frame
CSS contains required debug overlay rules
integration_checks: LoopDeps type accepts new optional debug fields without breaking existing tests
Render loop test still passes with no debug callback set
Build succeeds with no type errors
manual_checks: Default load (no query param): no overlay visible
Load with ?debug: overlay appears top-left with live values
Mode name changes when mode switches
Overlay doesn't block pointer interaction (pointer-events: none)

risks.risk: Adding fields to LoopDeps could break existing render loop tests
risks.mitigation: All new fields are optional (? suffix), so existing code and tests remain compatible

risks.risk: Debug overlay DOM updates could cause layout thrash affecting FPS
risks.mitigation: Throttle updates to ~4Hz and use textContent (not innerHTML) to minimize reflow

definition_of_done: Debug overlay enabled only via `?debug` query param — disabled by default
Shows FPS, active mode name, point count, bass value, treble value
Does not display raw visitor identifiers (IP, UA, geo, seed)
No console errors on normal load
Scene works with and without autoplay
Mute toggle and info overlay still work
